meaning: n.<澳俚>假报道,无稽之谈,谎言,谣传
examples: In truth, the whole story was a complete furphy, and many people in the news media were conned.
The firm's team was led by Sydney partner David Templeman and included partners Melita Cottrell and Baden Furphy, based in Sydney and Melbourne respectively.
Joe Furphy said he was concerned that fishermen on the lough were suffering more than anyone else and they were not responsible for the damage to the modiolus.
